hero.title: Jura Champi
hero.subtitle: Fresh mushroom producer in the Jura region

story.title: Our Story
story.paragraph1: Jura Champi is a small artisan farm where we grow our mushrooms in accordance with organic farming and strive to preserve the nature and environment around us. Located in the small village of Collonges, on the edge of the Jura. Our small farm is in the Ain department, close to the Swiss border.
story.learnMore: Discover our story and where to find us →

clients.title: Who are our mushrooms for?
clients.intro: Our mushrooms are for restaurateurs, caterers, communities, organic food stores and other businesses. Individuals can also be delivered directly to their homes.
clients.restaurantsDesc: Fresh, quality products to enhance your dishes
clients.canteensDesc: Regular supplies for local, healthy meals
clients.individualsDesc: Home delivery for your everyday recipes

productsPreview.title: Discover our selection of fresh mushrooms grown at the foot of the Jura

products.title: Our Mushrooms
products.subtitle: Fresh mushrooms grown with passion at the foot of the Jura

pleuroteBlanche.name: White oyster mushroom
pleuroteBlanche.description: The white oyster mushroom offers a delicate texture and subtle, mild flavor. Ideal pan-fried, in sauce or in risottos.

pleurotePanicaut.name: King oyster mushroom (Pleurotus eryngii)
pleurotePanicaut.description: King oyster belongs to the large oyster family — among the best-known edible mushrooms. It stands out for its smaller, fleshy brownish cap and chunky stem; the inner side of the cap is white.
pleurotePanicaut.origin: Its name comes from the wild plant sea holly (Eryngium); the mushroom grows on dead stumps. Also known as Eryngii. Native to southern Europe, especially Spain and southern France.
pleurotePanicaut.taste: Mild but delicious. Firm, thick white flesh with a pleasant aroma.
pleurotePanicaut.cuisine: Very versatile: seared slices with oil, salt and pepper, or with cream and vegetables; stews; pasta and rice with tomato and chopped mushroom; also raw in sandwiches or salads. Keeps well when stored cool.
pleurotePanicaut.benefits: Like other oyster mushrooms, it may support immunity, white blood cell production, fibre for gut balance, antioxidants, anti-inflammatory effects and the nervous system.
pleurotePanicaut.properties: Rich in vitamins and minerals: B2, B3, B5, iron, phosphorus, copper, potassium, etc.

blackPearl.name: Black Pearl
blackPearl.description: An oyster-mushroom variety with a dark cap and meaty texture, valued for its bold flavour.
blackPearl.origin: A recent variety from hybridizing European and Japanese grey oyster strains.

shiitake.name: Shiitake
shiitake.description: Also called the fragrant mushroom: a large cap from light to dark brown, sometimes speckled white, 5–15 cm across, soft and silky. In the wild it grows in broadleaf forests. The name comes from Japanese, linked to the shii tree (Castanopsis), related to oak and beech.
shiitake.origin: Asian in origin; traditionally cultivated in China and Japan.
shiitake.taste: Meaty and savoury, deepens dishes. Aromas reminiscent of onion and sulphurous vegetables (cooked cabbage, leeks…).
shiitake.cuisine: Cook 15–20 minutes (20 is ideal). Pan-fried or in broth.
shiitake.benefits: Lentinan, an immunostimulant from shiitake, has been studied for limiting fat buildup in the liver and supporting liver function.
shiitake.properties: May support immunity and natural defences. Rich in antioxidants for cell and skin ageing. Nutrient-rich: potassium, manganese, magnesium, iron, phosphorus; vitamins D, B1, B2, B6, B12, B3; lipids and protein. Used in cooking and traditional wellness contexts.

criniereLion.name: Lion's mane
criniereLion.description: Also called bearded tooth mushroom: unique stringy texture and delicate notes. Delicious pan-fried or in broth.

history.title: Our story
history.paragraph1: Jura Champi is a small artisan farm where we grow our mushrooms in accordance with organic farming and strive to preserve the nature and environment around us. Located in the small village of Collonges, on the edge of the Jura. Our small farm is in the Ain department, close to the Swiss border.

premises.title: Our premises
premises.description: Our farm is located in Collonges along the Jura. Our mushrooms are grown in accordance with organic farming.

online.title: Online & by phone

markets.title: At markets
markets.description: Come say hello and taste our mushrooms at local producer markets. A chance to chat, discover our latest products and leave with a full basket!
